threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Overground' instead of just 'The Train' like everyone else around here. Those shiny orange trains won't make you a local.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week; a guy was passionately explaining the nuances of drum and bass to a half-asleep passenger. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The unmistakable scent of grilled kebab wafting through the streets on a Friday night, mixed with the sound of street performers—it's the heartbeat of Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a nightclub that serves breakfast until 3 PM the next day, blurring the lines between raves and brunch plans.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their choice of burrito place—if they’re asking for extra rice at La Oaxaqueña, they’re just passing through.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 19 Polk bus arguing with himself about the best slice of pizza in the city. No one batted an eye, just the Tenderloin way.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but locals know it’s the hidden tascas and the smell of fresh pa amb tomàquet that really define us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confused look when they try to navigate the Metro. They always hesitate at ticket machines, especially on weekends.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look at the bus timetable instead of just checking your phone. Ain't nobody got time for that around here!
Caught a night bus home and a guy was arguing with a pigeon. 'I’m not afraid of you!' he yelled. The pigeon just stared. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a pack of kids using the U-Bahn ticket machines like it's an arcade game, pressing buttons like pros. This is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they have spontaneous techno parties in the middle of a park, complete with a makeshift DJ booth made from recycled pallets.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they hold their breath until the BART train shows up—never trusting the arrival times posted.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 this morning; a guy confidently recited Shakespeare to no one in particular while the bus lurched past a steam vent. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas where locals gather for afternoon vermut and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by their confusion over the 4-way intersections — they stop at every corner, waiting for traffic lights that don't exist.
